note: v2 resubmission after §8.6 gate failure. Largest sub-group M04-B: 273/797 substantive verses = 34.3%, below 40% threshold. All 58 terms covered. 43 STAYS terms routed to 10 substantive sub-groups. 15 BOUNDARY terms routed to M04-BOUNDARY.

subgroups.label: Exultation in YHWH: Vertical Rejoicing Directed Toward God
subgroups.core_description: OT register of inner exultation directed explicitly toward YHWH as the object and ground of joy. The soul exults in YHWH's salvation, the heart rejoices in God's holy name, God's own inner delight is directed toward his people. Core OT vertical-exultation vocabulary: gil_verb, gil, su.s, cha.dah, re.na.nah, te.ev.

subgroups.label: Communal and Festive Rejoicing: Gladness in Worship, Feast, and Celebration
subgroups.core_description: OT dominant communal-festive-worship register. Rejoicing before God at appointed feasts commanded for entire households; temple celebrations; divinely produced and socially expansive gladness. Terms sa.mach (147), sim.chah (89), sa.me.ach (21), ma.s.vo.s (16) are routed here as primary.

subgroups.label: NT Joy in Christ and the Spirit: Gladness of the Gospel Community
subgroups.core_description: Distinctively NT register: inner gladness arising from believing in Christ, from the indwelling Spirit, and from the gospel community. Holy Spirit named as agent or source of joy. Rejoicing in the Lord as constant commanded disposition. Core NT terms: chairo (58), chara (57), plus smaller Greek joy terms.

subgroups.label: Shared Communal Rejoicing: Joy as Solidarity and Co-Celebration
subgroups.core_description: Register where rejoicing is constitutively shared — sought out, invited into, and completed in community. Sunchairo defines the register: the shepherd calling neighbours to co-rejoice, the body members sharing gladness when one is honoured, love co-rejoicing with truth.

subgroups.label: Promised and Eschatological Joy: Gladness of Restoration and the Suffering-Paradox
subgroups.core_description: Two closely related registers: (1) forward-oriented gladness promised as reversal of sorrow through divine redemption; (2) NT paradox of joy coexisting with and arising from suffering. Both orient joy toward what is not yet fully present. OT primary term: sa.s.von (22). NT bridging term: agalliao (11).

subgroups.label: Cheerfulness Under Adversity: Fragile Inner Comfort Amid Suffering
subgroups.core_description: Small but distinct register of cheerfulness as a desired or attempted inner state in the face of extreme adversity — fragile, barely attainable, or conspicuously absent. Distinct from M04-E's gospel-grounded suffering-joy by vulnerability and tenuousness rather than hope-confidence.

subgroups.label: Delight in God's Word, Law, and Wisdom: Pleasure as Inner Orientation Toward the Divine
subgroups.core_description: Affective inner pleasure taken specifically in God's word, law, testimonies, statutes, wisdom, and in YHWH himself as its direct object. Psalm 119 series is the anchor (sha.a.shu.im, sha.a). Distinguished from M04-H (volitional-will) by affective-pleasurable character.

subgroups.label: Volitional Delight: Inner Pleasure as Sovereign Will and Directed Desire
subgroups.core_description: Register in which cha.phets, che.phets, and eudokeo function primarily as markers of inner volitional disposition: willing-pleasure, sovereign purpose, directed inner resolve. Spans divine sovereignty (God's delight as his will governing history), divine pleasure in persons, human volitional willing, and negated-pleasure marking rejection.

subgroups.label: Wonder at God's Marvellous Works: Awe Before the Extraordinary
subgroups.core_description: Inner response of awe, marvel, and astonishment directed at God's extraordinary deeds, creative works, and the incomprehensibility of his ways. The contemplative and perceptual character distinguishes this from gladness. Includes wonder-as-proclamation, wonder-as-incomprehensibility, and wonder-as-dark-astonishment.

subgroups.label: Pleasantness and Relational Delight: The Agreeableness of Persons, Bonds, and Creation
subgroups.core_description: Experiential register of pleasantness as a quality of what is agreeable, beautiful, and nourishing to the inner person. Receptive rather than exultant character. Spans divine beauty (no.am — the Lord's own being), relational warmth (na.em — Jonathan's companionship in the soul), communal harmony (na.im — brotherly unity), and gracious speech (no.am — honey to soul and body).

subgroups.label: Boundary Terms
subgroups.core_description: Structural holding sub-group for 15 terms designated BOUNDARY at Phase 3. Corpora did not clearly confirm M04 alignment, or were too small, mixed, or uncertain. No characteristic-bearing analysis at Phase 5. Researcher disposition required at Phase 12.

verse_assignments_by_term.note: All is_relevant vc rows for each mti_term_id route to the sub-group listed below. This is the complete authoritative routing for all 58 active terms. Every active is_relevant verse is covered via its term's primary sub-group assignment.
